title: Network Evidence, Safety, and Troubleshooting Method · Network Control Room
summary: Operate a safe virtual help-desk lab with tickets, diagrams, baselines, commands, packet evidence, and change records. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a reproducible network evidence notebook while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply identify, theorize, test, plan, implement or escalate, verify, prevent, and document steps to one network incident.
Separate user report, observed symptom, affected scope, recent change, and reproducible condition before forming a theory.
Form one falsifiable probable-cause theory and isolate it with bottom-up, top-down, or divide-and-conquer evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-incident-documentation-1ksybx" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Incident Documentation into observable performance evidence.

stimulus.title: Net Incident Documentation failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Command history alone is a complete and understandable incident record.

lines.label: required

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Net Incident Documentation condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Incident Documentation applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record timeline, environment, commands, findings, actions, outcomes, lessons, and evidence without exposing secrets.
